channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Former MLB Commissioner Fay Vincent, who played major role in Pete Rose, George Steinbrenner bans, dead at 86
item.description: Former MLB Commissioner Fay Vincent, who played major roles in Pete Rose's and George Steinbrenner's lifetime bans, died Saturday at age 86.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fans at Raptors game continue trend of booing US national anthem at pro sporting events in Canada
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Fans at a Toronto Raptors game continued an emerging trend Sunday of booing the American national anthem at pro sporting events in Canada.
item.dc:creator: IAN HARRISON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Luka Doncic heads to the Lakers and Anthony Davis goes to the Mavs in blockbuster trade
item.description: Luka Doncic led Dallas to the NBA Finals last season. Anthony Davis won a championship with the Los Angeles Lakers. They now have switched teams in an absolute blockbuster.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fay Vincent, baseball commissioner during three years of turmoil, dies at 86
item.description: Fay Vincent, who became baseball commissioner in 1989 and then was forced out three years later by owners intent on a labor confrontation with players, has died.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Humbled: Erling Haaland goaded by Gabriel and teenager Lewis-Skelly in spiky Arsenal-City game
item.description: Erling Haaland experienced an uncomfortable return match against Arsenal in the Premier League on Sunday, with two opponents — including a teenager — teasing him after goals and the crowd at Emirates Stadium reminding the striker of his provocative comments in the teams' last meeting.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Once the plucky underdog, the Kansas City Chiefs have become the NFL juggernaut fans love to hate
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Kansas City Chiefs were once the loveable NFL underdogs, led by the good-natured coach who couldn’t win the big one, the plucky quarterback with the Kermit the Frog-like voice, and the superstar tight end with charisma to spare.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: DeWanna Bonner will join the Indiana Fever, AP source says
item.description: DeWanna Bonner is headed to Indiana to join Caitlin Clark and the Fever, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Sunday. The person spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity because no official announcement has been made.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Carolina retires jersey number of three-time WNBA MVP A’ja Wilson
item.description: Three-time WNBA MVP A’ja Wilson had her South Carolina No. 22 jersey hung in the rafters at Colonial Life Arena on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Pete Iacobelli, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why Bobby Hurley was half right for having Arizona State avoid handshakes after loss to Arizona
item.description: Hurley ordered his players off the court to avoid a conflagration after BJ Freeman head-butted Arizona's Caleb Love.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Ohtani's return to pitching could come in May for World Series champion Dodgers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani pitching for the Los Angeles Dodgers in May is "about right,” manager Dave Roberts said Saturday at the team's annual fan fest. He reiterated that Ohtani won't pitch in the season-opening Japan Series against the Chicago Cubs on March 18-19 in Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: David Benavidez dominates David Morell with unanimous decision win to unify light heavyweight title
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — David Benavidez unified the light heavyweight championship by earning a unanimous decision over challenger David Morrell on Saturday night to keep his interim WBC belt and win the WBA title.
item.dc:creator: W.G. RAMIREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Blackhawks record: Landon Slaggert scores 7 seconds into a game against Florida
item.description: Landon Slaggert scored seven seconds into Chicago's game at the Panthers on Saturday afternoon — the fastest goal to start a game ever for the Blackhawks, according to the NHL record book.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Despite Alex Ovechkin scoring his 877th goal, the Capitals lose to the Jets 5-4 in overtime
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Alex Ovechkin scored the 877th goal of his career to move 18 away from breaking the NHL record, but the Washington Capitals lost 5-4 in overtime to the Winnipeg Jets on Saturday night in a showdown of the top two teams in the NHL that lived up to that billing.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Self goes for comic relief after No. 11 Kansas blows biggest lead in loss in program history
item.description: WACO, Texas (AP) — Kansas blowing the biggest lead in a loss in the storied program's history didn't cost coach Bill Self his sense of humor.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arizona State coach Bobby Hurley waves players off court after testy finish against rival Arizona
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Tommy Lloyd and Bobby Hurley met near midcourt during pregame, exchanging pleasantries, patting each other on the back. An afternoon of chirping and a late headbutt sent the coaches in opposite directions by the time the rivalry game was over.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Travis Kelce fined for taunting Bills on Patrick Mahomes touchdown after not being penalized during game
item.description: Travis Kelce was fined $11,255 for taunting during an AFC championship game victory over the Buffalo Bills, but he wasn't penalized during the game for the stunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rangers' J.T. Miller scores in opening period in return to team day after acquired in trade
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — J.T. Miller’s return to the New York Rangers got off to a fast start when he scored two goals Saturday in a 6-3 loss to the Boston Bruins, a day after he was acquired in a trade from Vancouver.
item.dc:creator: KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CMU Baseball Ethan Nunez gears up for sophomore year
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Just a year ago, Ethan Nunez had no college offers. Now, the Colorado Mesa infielder holds one of the highest honors in the Rocky Mountain Athletic Conference (RMAC). “I couldn’t believe it, honestly,” Nunez said.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Clippers swap pair of veteran players with Utah Jazz
item.description: The Los Angeles Clippers have dealt two veteran players to the Utah Jazz for another pair of veterans. P.J.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Jazz trade Mills and Eubanks for Bamba and Tucker
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Five days before the NBA trade deadline, the Utah Jazz have made a deal with the Los Angeles Clippers. The Jazz have acquired center Mo Bamba, forward P.J.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Scottie Scheffler makes miraculous par save on 18th hole at Pebble Beach after errant tee shot
item.description: Scottie Scheffler had a miraculous par save on the 18th hole at Pebble Beach on Friday after hitting his tee shot onto the beach, leaving slim odds at a good outcome.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Super Bowl bettors in California have a workaround
item.description: Though Californians looking to place bets on the upcoming Super Bowl LIX are limited by state law banning the practice, a loophole appears to let bettors wager legally.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Rowdies Forrest Lasso returns to field after season-ending injury
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) - The Tampa Bay Rowdies returned to the field this week for preseason and so did veteran defender Forrest Lasso. After scoring two goals May 18, 2024, Lasso was named Player of the Week for his performance.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ha-Seong Kim agrees to 2-year deal worth $29 million with Rays, AP source says
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rays and free agent Ha-Seong Kim have agreed to a two-year contract valued at $29 million, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NFL kicker Justin Tucker accused of sexual misconduct by 6 masseuses: report
item.description: Justin Tucker, kicker for the Baltimore Ravens, has been accused by six massage therapists at four separate businesses of sexually inappropriate behavior, the Baltimore Banner reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: McDonald's announces Angel Reese Special, set to debut in February
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Angel Reese is set to add another first to her list of celebrity endorsements and accomplishments. In a post on X, fast food mega-chain McDonald's announced "The Angel Reese Special," will debut on Feb. 10 in collaboration with the Chicago Sky's star power forward.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: No Super Bowl rally if Kansas City Chiefs beat Philadelphia Eagles: Sources
item.description: Multiple sources have confirmed that should the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl LIX, there will be no rally for the public.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Dwyane Wade reveals cancer diagnosis, kidney surgery
item.description: The Chicago native shared the story of the "shocking" diagnosis and the emotional toll it took on him on the latest episode of his 'The Why' podcast.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, NewsNation

item.title: Crosby's overtime goal beats Utah HC, 3-2
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Sidney Crosby scored with 1:06 left in overtime as the Pittsburgh Penguins beat the Utah Hockey Club 3-2 at Delta Center on Wednesday. It was Crosby's 95th career game-winning goal, moving him past Steve Yzerman for the 13th most in NHL history.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Boys Basketball: San Marcos 82, Poway 48
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys Basketball at Poway on Tuesday evening with the Titans hosting San Marcos. The Titans were down 22-18 at the end of the 1st, but down 13 at the break and San Marcos kept the pedal to the metal and pulled off in the 2nd half and won 82-48.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Scottie Scheffler details his hand injury from making ravioli and is ready to get back to golf
item.description: PEBBLE BEACH, Calif. (AP) — Scottie Scheffler felt frustrated sitting at home for two tournaments he normally plays while recovering from glass puncturing the palm of his right hand while making ravioli. A big part of him was thankful the injury wasn't worse.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Warriors to retire Andre Iguodala's No. 9 jersey
item.description: As Andre Iguodala moves around town in the Bay Area these days, he is regularly stopped by fans eager to praise him for how he carried himself while playing for the Golden State Warriors.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Alyssa Thomas heading to Mercury from Sun in trade, AP source says
item.description: The Phoenix Mercury were finalizing a deal to acquire Alyssa Thomas from the Connecticut Sun, a person familiar with the trade told The Associated Press on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lions agree to hire Broncos' John Morton as OC and promote Kelvin Sheppard to DC, AP source says
item.description: The Detroit Lions have agreed to hire Denver Broncos assistant John Morton as offensive coordinator and promote Kelvin Sheppard to defensive coordinator, according to a person familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Golden State Warriors to retire Andre Iguodala’s jersey number
item.description: (KRON) – The Golden State Warriors announced on Tuesday that the team will be retiring four-time NBA champion and 2015 Finals MVP Andre Iguodala’s No. 9 jersey.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Taylor Swift watches boyfriend Travis Kelce and the Chiefs beat the Bills and get back to Super Bowl
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — You could say that Taylor Swift's championship era has reached its second consecutive season.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Report: Bears hiring Declan Doyle as offensive coordinator
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — On the same evening the Chicago Bears are reportedly hiring their next defensive coordinator, the Bears have also committed to a new offensive coordinator under first-year head coach Ben Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
